owner.description: Repository owner (user or organization login). Legacy accepted a single "owner/repo" repository config value and split it at runtime; the engine's path templating urlencodes each {{ }} reference as one opaque path segment (a literal "/" would be percent-encoded, not treated as a segment delimiter), so owner and repo are declared as two separate config keys instead.

repo.description: Repository name (without the owner prefix). See owner's description for why this is a separate config key from legacy's single repository field.

base_url.description: GitHub API base URL override for tests or GitHub Enterprise.

installation_id.description: GitHub App installation ID for auth_type=github_app.

installation_repositories.description: Optional comma-separated repository names for a restricted installation token.

installation_repository_ids.description: Optional comma-separated repository IDs for a restricted installation token.

installation_permissions.description: Optional JSON object of requested GitHub App installation-token permissions.

public_access.description: Explicit opt-in for unauthenticated (public) reads. Set to any non-empty value (e.g. 'true') to allow reads with no token/app credentials configured. Leaving this unset (and auth_type unset/not one of the public synonyms below) requires a token secret or app_id+installation_id+private_key to authenticate; a config with none of those AND no explicit public opt-in hard-errors rather than silently falling back to an unauthenticated read (F4 fail-loud deviation from legacy's auth_type=auto resolution, which DOES silently fall through to public — see docs.md Known limits/ledger G14). This is the PRIMARY documented opt-in surface (the resulting behavior — explicit opt-in required, no silent fallthrough — is what the legacy behavior SHOULD have been from a fail-loud standpoint); auth_type (below) is an additional, purely-additive way to reach the identical mode:none outcome for callers migrating a legacy auth_type=public-shaped config verbatim.

auth_type.description: Legacy's auth_type string-enum surface (auth.go:81-82), restored ADDITIONALLY alongside public_access once engine.ResolveCheckWhen made the when-grammar's `in` operator statically validatable (S3 engine mini-wave item 2, ledger G14 R1 CONDITION). Only the 4 legacy PUBLIC synonyms are reproduced here — 'public', 'none', 'anonymous', or 'unauthenticated' (case-sensitive; legacy lowercases/normalizes before comparing, this bundle does not) arm the identical mode:none candidate public_access arms. Legacy's OTHER auth_type values (token/oauth/github_app/etc. synonyms, and auth_type=github_app's override-token-precedence behavior) are NOT reproduced — this key exists solely to close the public-enum migration gap, not to replicate legacy's full auth_type mode-selection semantics; any other auth_type value (or none at all) has zero effect on auth selection, which still resolves via the token/app_id-presence candidates ahead of it in the auth list.

since.description: Lower-bound timestamp for issues, issue_comments, and pull_request_review_comments (incremental start; also usable as a fresh-sync start_config_key value).

token.description: GitHub token: classic PAT, fine-grained PAT, OAuth token, GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN, or a pre-generated installation access token. Used only for Bearer auth; never logged.

private_key.description: GitHub App PEM private key for auth_type=github_app. Never logged.

private_key_base64.description: Base64-encoded GitHub App PEM private key for auth_type=github_app (alternative to private_key). Never logged.
